message:
test(serve): improve coverage for ValidateConfig, checkWSOrigin, extractAuthClaims (bd-1j1a5)

ValidateConfig 68→100%, checkWSOrigin 63→86%, extractAuthClaims 60→100%.
Tests cover OIDC/mTLS/API-key validation branches, WS origin CORS checks,
and auth context extraction edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1iu4x","title":"Unit tests for internal/serve HTTP handlers","description":"# Serve Package Test Coverage

## Current State
- Coverage: 27.6% (27.8% after recent commits)
- Primary files: server.go, handlers.go, scanner.go, websocket.go
- Role: HTTP API server, WebSocket, SSE for real-time updates

## Already Tested (recent commits)
- `toJSONMap` - JSON round-trip conversion
- `isLoopbackHost` - Loopback IP detection
- `originAllowed` - CORS origin validation
- `requestIDFromContext` - Context extraction
- Committed: 1cc65fc2

## Target Functions (0% coverage)

### server.go
- `parseJWT(token string) (*JWTClaims, error)` - JWT parsing (needs mock)
- Config validation helpers

### scanner.go (all 0%)
- `handleScannerStatus` - HTTP handler
- `handleRunScan` - HTTP handler
- `runScanAsync` - Background scan
- `handleScannerHistory` - HTTP handler
- `handleListFindings` - HTTP handler
- `handleGetFinding` - HTTP handler
- `handleDismissFinding` - HTTP handler
- `handleCreateBeadFromFinding` - HTTP handler
- `handleListBugs` - HTTP handler
- `handleBugsSummary` - HTTP handler
- `handleBugsNotify` - HTTP handler
- `publishScannerEvent` - SSE publisher

### websocket.go
- WebSocket upgrade handlers
- Message routing

## Strategy
1. **Pure helpers first** (already done)
2. **httptest handlers** - Use httptest.NewRecorder for handler tests
3. **Mock dependencies** - Create interfaces for scanner, state access

## Implementation Pattern
```go
func TestHandleScannerStatus(t *testing.T) {
    req := httptest.NewRequest("GET", "/api/scanner/status", nil)
    w := httptest.NewRecorder()
    handler := http.HandlerFunc(handleScannerStatus)
    handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    // Assert response
}
```

## Risks
- Scanner handlers depend on external UBS binary
- WebSocket tests need connection mocking
- Some handlers need auth context

## Target Coverage
- Current: 27.8%
- Target: 50-60%","notes":"Coverage pushed from 34.6% to 55.1% (20.5pp gain). Added 150+ httptest unit tests covering safety, pipelines, mail, reservations, approvals, openapi, memory handlers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletMoose","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:33:49.987179337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:53:28.965031944Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:53:28.964901702Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iu4x","depends_on_id":"bd-p2pr8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:40:23.201263541Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"bd-1iu4x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PROGRESS (2026-02-05): Pure helper tests complete (commit 1cc65fc2): toJSONMap, isLoopbackHost, originAllowed, requestIDFromContext.  {"id":"bd-1iznv","title":"Task: Implement mode badges, tier chips, and colors","description":"# Task: Implement mode badges, tier chips, and colors

## Purpose
Visual distinction for reasoning modes in dashboard and synthesizer views.

## Scope
- Mode badge: short code (A1, F43) + icon
- Tier chip: CORE / ADV / EXP with subtle color palette
- Category color: map A–L to a consistent palette
- Status color: pending/active/done overlays

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/ui_styles.go
func ModeBadge(mode ReasoningMode) string
func TierChip(tier ModeTier) string
func CategoryColor(cat ModeCategory) lipgloss.Color
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mode badge includes code + icon
- [ ] Tier chip shows CORE/ADV/EXP
- [ ] Colors consistent across dashboard + synthesizer
- [ ] Works in ASCII fallback mode

## Dependencies
- Ensemble panel (bd-121pa)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:38.468762804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:59:53.454493698Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:59:53.454474602Z","close_reason":"Added mode/tier UI helpers and hooked in dashboard","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iznv","depends_on_id":"bd-121pa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1j956","title":"RCH: Implement DCG whitelist integration for RCH commands","description":"Ensure RCH-related commands are whitelisted in DCG.

## Background

RCH intercepts build commands and may transform them:
- Original: `cargo build`
- Transformed: `rch build cargo -- build`

DCG might block rch commands if not whitelisted.

## Integration Points

### 1. Whitelist RCH Commands
Add to DCG whitelist:
```
rch *             # All rch commands
rch build *       # Build commands
rch intercept *   # Intercept invocations
```

### 2. Pass-Through Logic
When DCG encounters an RCH-wrapped command, check inner command against blocklist.

### 3. Code Location
- internal/integrations/coordinator.go
- Document in DCG config section

## Acceptance Criteria

1. DCG whitelist integration for RCH commands
2. rch build and rch offload whitelisted
3. Inner command validation works
4. Config option to enable/disable integration
5. Unit tests for whitelist matching
6. E2E test verifying RCH commands not blocked","acceptance_criteria":"1. DCG whitelist integration for RCH commands
2. rch build and rch offload whitelisted
3. Config option to enable/disable integration
4. Unit tests for whitelist matching
5. E2E test verifying RCH commands not blocked","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:08:07.746048142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:48:57.772347493Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T03:48:57.772273546Z","close_reason":"Implemented DCG whitelist + RCH passthrough","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1j956","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1j956","depends_on_id":"bd-3u84u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1j956","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jiuu","title":"DCG: Implement audit logging for blocked commands","description":"Log all blocked commands to audit file for review and debugging.

## Requirements

1. Log every blocked command with:
   - Timestamp (RFC3339)
   - Command attempted
   - Agent/pane that attempted it
   - DCG rule that triggered block
   - Session ID

2. Log format: JSONL (one JSON object per line)

3. Rotation: rotate when file exceeds 10MB

## Log Entry Schema

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "event": "command_blocked",
  "command": "rm -rf /important",
  "pane": "agent-1",
  "session": "my-project",
  "rule": "rm_recursive_dangerous",
  "dcg_output": "Blocked: rm -rf with dangerous path"
}
```

## Implementation

```go
package dcg

type AuditLogger struct {
    path     string
    file     *os.File
    mu       sync.Mutex
    maxBytes int64
}

func (l *AuditLogger) LogBlocked(cmd string, pane string, rule string, output string) error {
    // Write JSONL entry
    // Check rotation
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/dcg/audit.go

## Testing
- Log blocked command, verify entry written
- Fill log to rotation threshold, verify new file created
- Concurrent logging (multiple agents blocking simultaneously)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:29.829975966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:16:27.591217606Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:16:27.591053347Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jiuu","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jiuu","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1zs9y","title":"Feature: Token Budget Advisor","description":"## Summary
Estimate token usage for selected modes and provide budget-aware recommendations before running an ensemble.

## Rationale
Token costs can be significant; users need visibility and alternatives.

## Implementation
- Per-mode estimation using token heuristics (bd-2t4cs) + historical calibration.
- `ntm ensemble estimate` shows per-mode breakdown + total estimate.
- `--budget` flag warns and suggests cheaper alternatives.
- Track actual vs. estimated to improve calibration over time.

## Logging
- Log estimates, calibration deltas, and budget warnings.

## Tests
- Unit: estimation accuracy + calibration adjustment
- Integration: budget warnings with sample configs
- E2E: estimate output + budget warning path

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reasonably accurate estimates
- [ ] Per-mode breakdown available
- [ ] Budget warnings fire at thresholds
- [ ] Alternative mode suggestions
- [ ] Calibration improves over time

## Dependencies
- Budget control + tracking (bd-1dt84, bd-3d4tl)
- Token estimation heuristics (bd-2t4cs)
- Mode catalog (bd-11mtt)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:09:08.646964343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:47:34.424225995Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-2t4cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4tl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-209g","title":"Add Agent Mail integration - auto-register agents with MCP Agent Mail on spawn","description":"## Overview
Auto-register spawned agents with MCP Agent Mail for coordination.

## Implementation
When agents spawn, NTM:
1. Calls `mcp__mcp-agent-mail__ensure_project` with workspace path
2. For each agent, calls `mcp__mcp-agent-mail__register_agent` with:
   - program: 'claude-code' | 'codex-cli' | 'gemini-cli'
   - model: agent model
   - task_description: from spawn config or 'General development'

## Agent Names
Uses adjective+noun format per Agent Mail rules (e.g., 'GreenCastle', 'BlueLake').
Maps pane -> agent name for later reference.

## Error Handling
```go
type AgentMailError struct {
    Phase    string  // "ensure_project", "register_agent", "health_check"
    AgentIdx int     // Which agent failed (-1 for project-level)
    Cause    error
}
```

### Scenarios
1. **Agent Mail server not running**
   - Detect via health_check timeout (2s)
   - Log warning: "Agent Mail unavailable, skipping registration"
   - Continue spawn without registration
   - Set session flag: `agent_mail_available = false`

2. **ensure_project fails**
   - Log warning with error details
   - Continue spawn without registration
   - Retry on next operation

3. **register_agent fails for some agents**
   - Log warning for each failed agent
   - Continue with other agents
   - Return partial success in output

4. **Name collision**
   - Agent Mail auto-generates unique name
   - Store whatever name is returned

## Fallback Mode
When Agent Mail unavailable:
- File reservations disabled (warn user)
- Agent coordination disabled
- ntm assign still works (just no Agent Mail features)

## Output
```json
{
  "agent_mail": {
    "available": true,
    "project_registered": true,
    "agents_registered": 4,
    "agents_failed": 0,
    "agent_map": {
      "2": "GreenCastle",
      "3": "BlueLake",
      "4": "RedStone",
      "5": "PurpleBear"
    }
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Registers all agents on spawn
- [ ] Uses correct adjective+noun names
- [ ] Maps pane numbers to agent names
- [ ] Handles server unavailable gracefully (warning, continue)
- [ ] Handles partial registration failures
- [ ] Stores agent names in session state
- [ ] Falls back gracefully when unavailable
- [ ] Clear logging for all error scenarios

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Agent name generation
- Unit test: Pane-to-name mapping
- Unit test: Error handling for each scenario
- E2E test: Registration with real Agent Mail
- E2E test: Graceful degradation when unavailable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:36:38.950986689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:58:35.143387294Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:58:35.143294539Z","close_reason":"Implemented Agent Mail auto-registration on spawn. Added registerSpawnedAgents function in spawn.go that: (1) Ensures project exists via EnsureProject, (2) Creates unique agent identities for each spawned agent using CreateAgentIdentity, (3) Maps pane index to agent name, (4) Returns status in SpawnResponse JSON with available, project_registered, agents_registered, agents_failed, and agent_map fields. Also fixed pre-existing bugs: orphaned code in FormatRecoveryPrompt and missing UsagePercent field access in rotation.go.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-209g","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
